CytoGLMM 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • CytoGLMM Workflow for Pregnancy Dataset

CytoGLMM Workflow

Christof Seiler

Department of Statistics, Stanford University & Department of Data Science and Knowledge Engineering, Maastricht University

2021-02-18

Source: vignettes/CytoGLMM.Rmd
CytoGLMM.Rmd

Introduction

Flow and mass cytometry are important modern immunology tools for measuring expression levels of multiple proteins on single cells. The goal is to better understand the mechanisms of responses on a single cell basis by studying differential expression of proteins. Most current data analysis tools compare expressions across many computationally discovered cell types. Our goal is to focus on just one cell type. Differential analysis of marker expressions can be difficult due to marker correlations and inter-subject heterogeneity, particularly for studies of human immunology. We address these challenges with two multiple regression strategies: A generalized linear mixed model (GLMM) and a bootstrapped generalized linear model (GLM). Here, we illustrate the CytoGLMM R package and workflow for simulated mass cytometry data.

Prepare Simulated Data

Generate data from a Poisson GLM with random effects.

library("CytoGLMM")
library("magrittr")
set.seed(23)
df = generate_data()
df
## # A tibble: 16,000 x 12
##    donor condition   m01   m02   m03   m04   m05   m06   m07   m08   m09   m10
##    <int> <fct>     <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int>
##  1     1 treatment     2   127   116    17    17    17    56    60     7    78
##  2     1 treatment     0    96    54    29   169    32    39    11    19    55
##  3     1 treatment     1    14    52   182    26    12     7     6    37   274
##  4     1 treatment     6    20    34    21    42     3    30    19     7    68
##  5     1 treatment    18    30    15   139    22     4    10     5    32    19
##  6     1 treatment     1    17    38   151    14     5    26    29    26    83
##  7     1 treatment    24   178    10     8   498    21    26    33     8    26
##  8     1 treatment     1    83    16    59    85     2    11    28    11    28
##  9     1 treatment     2    24    48    13    27    17    51   194    28   225
## 10     1 treatment     2    39    13     4    36    28    39   104     1    60
## # … with 15,990 more rows

Extract the marker names from the data frame.

protein_names = names(df)[3:12]

Apply a data transformation.

df %<>% dplyr::mutate_at(protein_names, function(x) asinh(x/5))

GLMM

Fit model. It is normal to receive a warning about the covariance matrix estimate. This warning can be ignored.

glmm_fit = CytoGLMM::cytoglmm(df,
                              protein_names = protein_names,
                              condition = "condition",
                              group = "donor",
                              num_cores = 1)
glmm_fit
## number of cells per group and condition:   
##     control treatment
##   1    1000      1000
##   2    1000      1000
##   3    1000      1000
##   4    1000      1000
##   5    1000      1000
##   6    1000      1000
##   7    1000      1000
##   8    1000      1000
## 
## proteins included in the analysis:
##  m01 m02 m03 m04 m05 m06 m07 m08 m09 m10 
## 
## condition compared: condition 
## grouping variable: donor

Plot results.

plot(glmm_fit)

Summarize results.

summary(glmm_fit)
## # A tibble: 10 x 3
##    protein_name pvalues_unadj pvalues_adj
##    <chr>                <dbl>       <dbl>
##  1 m03              0.0000682    0.000682
##  2 m02              0.000163     0.000813
##  3 m01              0.000681     0.00227 
##  4 m06              0.155        0.388   
##  5 m05              0.371        0.646   
##  6 m08              0.388        0.646   
##  7 m04              0.512        0.731   
##  8 m09              0.859        0.954   
##  9 m10              0.874        0.954   
## 10 m07              0.954        0.954

Extract only proteins below an FDR of 0.05 from the \(p\)-value table.

summary(glmm_fit) %>% dplyr::filter(pvalues_adj < 0.05)
## # A tibble: 3 x 3
##   protein_name pvalues_unadj pvalues_adj
##   <chr>                <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1 m03              0.0000682    0.000682
## 2 m02              0.000163     0.000813
## 3 m01              0.000681     0.00227

GLM

Fit model.

glm_fit = CytoGLMM::cytoglm(df,
                            protein_names = protein_names,
                            condition = "condition",
                            group = "donor",
                            num_cores = 1,
                            num_boot = 1000)
glm_fit
## 
## #######################
## ## paired analysis ####
## #######################
## 
## number of bootstrap samples: 1000 
## 
## number of cells per group and condition:   
##     control treatment
##   1    1000      1000
##   2    1000      1000
##   3    1000      1000
##   4    1000      1000
##   5    1000      1000
##   6    1000      1000
##   7    1000      1000
##   8    1000      1000
## 
## proteins included in the analysis:
##  m01 m02 m03 m04 m05 m06 m07 m08 m09 m10 
## 
## condition compared: condition 
## grouping variable: donor

Plot results.

plot(glm_fit)

Summarize results.

summary(glm_fit)
## # A tibble: 10 x 3
##    protein_name pvalues_unadj pvalues_adj
##    <chr>                <dbl>       <dbl>
##  1 m03                  0.002      0.02  
##  2 m02                  0.006      0.03  
##  3 m01                  0.022      0.0733
##  4 m06                  0.394      0.885 
##  5 m04                  0.466      0.885 
##  6 m08                  0.544      0.885 
##  7 m09                  0.644      0.885 
##  8 m05                  0.708      0.885 
##  9 m10                  0.852      0.932 
## 10 m07                  0.932      0.932

Extract only proteins below an FDR of 0.05 from the \(p\)-value table.

summary(glm_fit) %>% dplyr::filter(pvalues_adj < 0.05)
## # A tibble: 2 x 3
##   protein_name pvalues_unadj pvalues_adj
##   <chr>                <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1 m03                  0.002        0.02
## 2 m02                  0.006        0.03

Session Info

sessionInfo()
## R version 3.6.2 (2019-12-12)
## Platform: x86_64-apple-darwin15.6.0 (64-bit)
## Running under: macOS  10.16
## 
## Matrix products: default
## BLAS:   /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/3.6/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib
## LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/3.6/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib
## 
## locale:
## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8
## 
## attached base packages:
## [1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base     
## 
## other attached packages:
## [1] magrittr_1.5     CytoGLMM_0.1.0   Rcpp_1.0.4.6     BiocStyle_2.14.4
## 
## loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
##  [1] nlme_3.1-144         fs_1.4.1             lubridate_1.7.4     
##  [4] doParallel_1.0.15    RColorBrewer_1.1-2   rprojroot_1.3-2     
##  [7] tools_3.6.2          backports_1.1.7      utf8_1.1.4          
## [10] R6_2.4.1             rpart_4.1-15         colorspace_1.4-1

## [16] compiler_3.6.2       cli_2.0.2            factoextra_1.0.6    
## [19] logging_0.10-108     sandwich_2.5-1       desc_1.2.0

## [46] fansi_0.4.1          munsell_0.5.0        abind_1.4-5

## [52] yaml_2.2.1           MASS_7.3-51.5        flexmix_2.3-15

## [61] bigmemory.sri_0.1.3  crayon_1.3.4         lattice_0.20-40     
## [64] cowplot_1.0.0        splines_3.6.2        knitr_1.28          
## [67] pillar_1.4.4         boot_1.3-24          reshape2_1.4.4      
## [70] codetools_0.2-16     stats4_3.6.2         glue_1.4.1

## [82] ggplot2_3.3.2        xfun_0.12            gower_0.2.1
